title: Tender Turkish Style Croatian Turkey Skillet
description: A rich and energizing Croatian dish featuring turkey, made in a traditional iron teppan skillet, with a blend of Turkish and Croatian flavors, this recipe is ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly and perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large iron teppan skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the ground turkey and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until it is no longer pink, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the tomato paste and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and cook, covered, until the liquid has been absorbed and the turkey is tender, about 10 minutes.
Stir in the chopped parsley and serve hot.
